retinal_sim - Full Validation Audit Report

Generated: 2026-04-07 12:38

Scope of This Report

This audit report shows the implemented validation checks, the exact pass criteria used, the observed outcomes, the assumptions and limitations attached to each check, and the code paths that produced the result. Statements here are evidence-backed for specific implemented checks in the current proof-of-concept build, not blanket claims of whole-simulator physiological or perceptual validation.

Summary

19/19 implemented validation checks passed under the current test harness for their stated scopes and assumptions
Claim calibration:
  • A passing report supports only the implemented checks and their declared claim scopes.
  • Internal consistency checks and external empirical alignment are reported separately and should not be conflated.
  • R3 scene-input semantics remain active: RGB-inferred scene spectra are not unique measured spectra unless the input mode says otherwise.
  • R4 retinal physiology provenance remains front-end-only evidence and does not imply full physiological or perceptual validation.
#TestResultArchitectureObserved
1Angular SubtensePASSArchitecture §11e Angular subtense correctnessw=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓ w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓ w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642°
2Retinal Scaling Across SpeciesPASSArchitecture §11e Retinal image scaling across specieshuman: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ cat: r
3Accommodation DefocusPASSArchitecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocushuman: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓ dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (exp
4Distance-Dependent Receptor SamplingPASSArchitecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor samplingd=1m → 51332 stimulated receptors d=2m → 9240 stimulated receptors d=4m → 2373 stimulated receptors d=8m → 575 stimulate
5Metamer PreservationPASSArchitecture §11a Metamer preservationcyan_blue_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0116 for [13, 150, 234] vs [52, 148, 235] ✓ teal_pair: max cone-catch rel diff
6RGB Round-TripPASSArchitecture §11a Roundtrip consistencyMax RMSE = 0.00
7Spectral Response PanelPASSArchitecture §11a Spectral response orderinghuman: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->M_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone human: sep(blue,yellow)=0
8MTF vs Diffraction LimitPASSArchitecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolutionMax |measured-predicted| = 0.0804
9PSF Energy ConservationPASSArchitecture §11b PSF energy conservationhuman: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓ dog: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓ cat: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓
10Pupil Throughput ScalingPASSArchitecture §2a Pupil throughput and aperture geometrytotal_energy={human=1024.0000, dog=1820.4448, cat=1592.8888}; ratios_vs_human={human=1.000, dog=1.778, cat=1.556}; max_r
11Cat Slit AnisotropyPASSArchitecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnosticssigma_x_px=2.149, sigma_y_px=1.011, FWHM_x_px=5, FWHM_y_px=3; psf_sigma_mm_x=0.0021, psf_sigma_mm_y=0.0010
12Wavelength-Dependent BlurPASSArchitecture §2b Longitudinal chromatic aberrationhuman: lca_400=+1.000D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-1.000D, min_sigma_at=555nm ✓ dog: lca_400=+0.750D, lca_555=+0.000D, lc
13Media Transmission DiagnosticsPASSArchitecture §2c Vitreous and media transmissionhuman: blue_mean=0.424, red_mean=0.990, source=C:\Users\steve\retinal_sim\retinal_sim\data\optical_media\human_reference
14Snellen AcuityPASSArchitecture §11c Snellen acuity predictionhuman=2.0′, dog=8.0′, cat=8.0′
15Dichromat ConfusionPASSArchitecture §11c Dichromat confusion axishuman medians: dog-conf=0.0374, cat-conf=0.1191, control=0.3653 dog medians: dog-conf=0.0301, cat-conf=0.0972, control=0
16Nyquist SamplingPASSArchitecture §11c Cone density to sampling limithuman: cone_density=200000/mm², spacing=2.40µm, nyquist=81.0 cpd (target 45-90) ✓ dog: cone_density=12000/mm², spacing=9
17Receptor CountPASSArchitecture §11c Receptor count validationhuman: measured=191176, expected=191233, count_err=0.000, cone_ratio_err=0.004 ✓ dog: measured=144374, expected=144474,
18Color Deficit ReproductionPASSArchitecture §11d Known visual deficit reproductionhuman Δ=0.2634, dog Δ=0.1602

Validation Framing

Validation Categories

Validation categoryResult count
analytic correctness3
external empirical alignment9
model self-consistency7

Claim Support Levels

Claim support levelResult count
moderate12
strong4
weak3
Legend: analytic correctness = closed-form or invariant checks; model self-consistency = internal implementation/regression checks; external empirical alignment = checks compared against local deterministic reference evidence.

Environment and Reproducibility

Report type
full_validation_audit
Stage scope
all
Species
human
Seed
42
Patch extent (deg)
1.0
Stimulus scale
0.01
Light level
photopic
Scene input mode
reflectance_under_d65
Scene spectrum source
RGB-inferred
Scene assumptions
RGB is interpreted as a display-referred color that is reconstructed into a Smits-inspired D65-optimized reflectance spectrum.; The recovered spectrum is inferred from RGB and is not a unique physical scene spectrum.; This mode models diffuse reflectance under D65 for comparative PoC work.
Repo commit
5fcf4f68e59d0fc156a88c95bfd583f65bfbcb17
Python
3.14.3
Platform
Windows-11-10.0.26200-SP0
NumPy
2.4.4
Retinal model scope
retinal_front_end_only
Retinal scope note
This simulation stops at the photoreceptor/retinal front end. It does not include post-receptoral, retinal-circuit, or cortical perception modeling.
Naka-Rushton confidence
low
Aperture weighting default
Disabled
Visual streak status
not_applicable
Validation categories
analytic correctness: 3, external empirical alignment: 9, model self-consistency: 7
Claim support levels
moderate: 12, strong: 4, weak: 3

Retinal Physiology Assumptions

Retinal Front-End Only. This simulation stops at the photoreceptor/retinal front end. It does not include post-receptoral, retinal-circuit, or cortical perception modeling.

Lambda-max source
Human lambda-max anchors follow the project architecture/spec reference set and are used with the unchanged Govardovskii A1 nomogram.
Lambda-max confidence
moderate
Lambda-max notes
Fixed species-reference peak wavelengths; not individualized or adaptation-dependent in-vivo fits.
Density-function source
Human density falloff is a proof-of-concept Gaussian approximation referencing Curcio 1990-style foveal/rod-free-zone anatomy.
Density-function confidence
moderate
Density-function notes
The current model is radially symmetric and patch-limited; it is not a full histological density map.
Naka-Rushton source
Human Naka-Rushton parameters are configurable retinal-front-end model parameters stored in YAML.
Naka-Rushton confidence
low
Naka-Rushton notes
Defaults preserve prior proof-of-concept behavior and should not be over-read as validated species-specific transduction measurements.
Naka-Rushton configuration
{
  "S_cone": {
    "n": 0.7,
    "sigma": 0.5,
    "R_max": 1.0
  },
  "M_cone": {
    "n": 0.7,
    "sigma": 0.5,
    "R_max": 1.0
  },
  "L_cone": {
    "n": 0.7,
    "sigma": 0.5,
    "R_max": 1.0
  },
  "rod": {
    "n": 1.0,
    "sigma": 0.1,
    "R_max": 1.0
  }
}
Aperture weighting
False (gaussian_prefilter)
Aperture notes
Optional retinal-front-end refinement; many human cone apertures remain sub-pixel at the current proof-of-concept grid scale.
Visual streak status
not_applicable
Visual streak notes
Human foveal modeling stays radially symmetric in this proof-of-concept build.

Simulation Output Families

These sections expose the R6 traceability path from scene assumptions to retinally delivered irradiance, then to photoreceptor sampling/activation, and finally to claim-calibrated comparative renderings.
Traceability path: scene/spectral assumptions -> optical-stage delivery -> retinal sampling and activation.
Example input
Representative deterministic RGB probe used to expose retinal irradiance, photoreceptor activation, and comparative rendering artifact families.
Scene input mode
reflectance_under_d65
Scene spectrum source
RGB-inferred
Retinal model scope
retinal_front_end_only
Naka-Rushton confidence
low
Validation framing
Comparative renderings below remain retinal-front-end outputs and are not perceptual reconstructions.

Retinal Irradiance Diagnostics

This family records where scene-spectrum assumptions and anterior-eye optical effects altered the retinally delivered light before receptor sampling.

Photoreceptor Activation Diagnostics

This family records where receptor sampling density, receptor classes, and front-end transduction changed the retained retinal information.

Overall summary

  "n_receptors": 38781,
  "mean_response": 0.3473074734210968,
  "median_response": 0.33241164684295654,
  "min_response": 0.0,
  "max_response": 0.7244668006896973
}

Retinal physiology summary

{
  "species": "human",
  "model_scope": "retinal_front_end_only",
  "scope_note": "This simulation stops at the photoreceptor/retinal front end. It does not include post-receptoral, retinal-circuit, or cortical perception modeling.",
  "lambda_max_values_nm": {
    "S_cone": 420.0,
    "M_cone": 530.0,
    "L_cone": 560.0,
    "rod": 498.0
  },
  "lambda_max_provenance": {
    "source": "Human lambda-max anchors follow the project architecture/spec reference set and are used with the unchanged Govardovskii A1 nomogram.",
    "confidence": "moderate",
    "notes": "Fixed species-reference peak wavelengths; not individualized or adaptation-dependent in-vivo fits."
  },
  "density_function_model": "radially_symmetric_gaussian",
  "density_function_provenance": {
    "source": "Human density falloff is a proof-of-concept Gaussian approximation referencing Curcio 1990-style foveal/rod-free-zone anatomy.",
    "confidence": "moderate",
    "notes": "The current model is radially symmetric and patch-limited; it is not a full histological density map."
  },
  "naka_rushton_configuration": {
    "S_cone": {
      "n": 0.7,
      "sigma": 0.5,
      "R_max": 1.0
    },
    "M_cone": {
      "n": 0.7,
      "sigma": 0.5,
      "R_max": 1.0
    },
    "L_cone": {
      "n": 0.7,
      "sigma": 0.5,
      "R_max": 1.0
    },
    "rod": {
      "n": 1.0,
      "sigma": 0.1,
      "R_max": 1.0
    }
  },
  "naka_rushton_provenance": {
    "source": "Human Naka-Rushton parameters are configurable retinal-front-end model parameters stored in YAML.",
    "confidence": "low",
    "notes": "Defaults preserve prior proof-of-concept behavior and should not be over-read as validated species-specific transduction measurements."
  },
  "aperture_weighting": {
    "enabled": false,
    "gaussian_sigma_scale": 0.5,
    "sigma_bin_px": 0.1,
    "truncate_sigma": 3.0,
    "method": "bilinear_only",
    "notes": "Optional retinal-front-end refinement; many human cone apertures remain sub-pixel at the current proof-of-concept grid scale.",
    "sigma_px_min": 0.0,
    "sigma_px_mean": 0.0,
    "sigma_px_max": 0.0,
    "approximation_note": "Optional retinal-front-end refinement; many human cone apertures remain sub-pixel at the current proof-of-concept grid scale.",
    "pixel_scale_mm": 0.016217429311160086
  },
  "visual_streak": {
    "supported": false,
    "enabled": false,
    "axis": "horizontal",
    "axis_ratio": 1.0,
    "status": "not_applicable",
    "notes": "Human foveal modeling stays radially symmetric in this proof-of-concept build."
  }
}

Comparative Renderings

These human-readable outputs are retinal-information renderings of the modeled retinal front end. They are not direct perceptual, retinal-circuit, or cortical reconstructions, and they should not be read as what the animal consciously sees.

Items

Comparative rendering: receptor-coded activation map

Voronoi rendering of receptor activations. Hue encodes receptor class and brightness encodes modeled activation.

{
  "id": "comparative_activation_map",
  "label": "Comparative rendering: receptor-coded activation map",
  "description": "Voronoi rendering of receptor activations. Hue encodes receptor class and brightness encodes modeled activation."
}

Retinal-information rendering

Regular-grid rendering of retained front-end activation information after retinal sampling.

{
  "id": "retinal_information_rendering",
  "label": "Retinal-information rendering",
  "description": "Regular-grid rendering of retained front-end activation information after retinal sampling."
}

External Reference Evidence

The tables below are explicit reference evidence used to calibrate R5 claims. They are reference expectations, not measured simulator outputs.

species acuity ranges

Reference evidence table. These rows describe external or configured expectations, not measured simulator outputs.

reference labelacuity arcminsourcebehavioral range arcmin
human1.0Snellen 20/20 standard; MAR = 1 arcmin[0.8, 1.2]
dog4.8Miller & Murphy 1995, approx 20/75; arch doc range 4-8 arcmin[4.0, 8.0]
cat7.5Blake et al. 1974, 20/100-20/200; MAR 5-10 arcmin; arch doc range 6-10 arcmin[6.0, 10.0]

density derived nyquist limits

Reference evidence table. These rows describe external or configured expectations, not measured simulator outputs.

reference labeltarget range cpdnominal cpddensity basissourceclaim note
human[45.0, 90.0]60.0Human foveal cone packing from the project density model, compared against architecture target values.retinal_sim_architecture.md §11c; project density-model expectation for human fovea.Reference band represents literature-aligned sampling expectations, not a direct behavioral acuity measurement.
dog[8.0, 20.0]12.0Dog area-centralis cone density interpreted through the hexagonal spacing approximation.retinal_sim_architecture.md §11c; dog area-centralis target range.Used as an externally grounded range for density-derived retinal sampling limits.
cat[8.0, 14.0]9.0Cat area-centralis cone density interpreted through the hexagonal spacing approximation.retinal_sim_architecture.md §11c; cat area-centralis target range.Reference band is approximate and should not be overread as full perceptual validation.

optical geometry expectations

Reference evidence table. These rows describe external or configured expectations, not measured simulator outputs.

reference labelpupil shapepupil area mm2focal length mmexpected optical consequencessourcevalue
humancircular7.068622.3['Smallest reference pupil area in the default species configs.', 'Largest retinal image magnification among the three modeled species.']Species optical config plus R1/R2 reporting assumptions.
dogcircular12.566417.0['Higher throughput than human under the default pupil geometry.', 'Smaller retinal image magnification than human because focal length is shorter.']Species optical config plus R1/R2 reporting assumptions.
catslit10.995618.5['Throughput exceeds human under the default slit approximation.', 'Axis-dependent blur is expected when slit anisotropy is active.', 'Retinal image magnification falls between dog and human from focal length alone.']Species optical config plus slit-pupil approximation used by the current optical stage.
claim_noteThese rows are reference expectations derived from modeled pupil geometry and focal length, not direct in-vivo measurements from the simulator.

wavelength transmission assumptions

Reference evidence table. These rows describe external or configured expectations, not measured simulator outputs.

reference labelexpected patternuv shortwave transmissionsourceclaim notevalue
humanBlue attenuation stronger than red transmission.most attenuated of the three species in the local reference tablesLocal ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.pyAssumption table reflects the configured reference transmission curve, not patient-specific ocular media.
dogBlue attenuation present, but less severe than the human reference curve.higher shortwave transmission than the human reference curveLocal ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.pyExternal alignment is to the configured species reference curve, not to a full age- or state-dependent physiological model.
catBlue attenuation present, with shortwave transmission distinct from both human and dog reference curves.higher shortwave transmission than the human reference curveLocal ocular-media CSV reference used by retinal_sim/optical/media.pyReference evidence documents configured assumptions about delivered spectra through the ocular media.
global_noteThese are report-layer reference assumptions used to calibrate claims about wavelength-dependent delivery to the retina.

Known Limitations / Deferred Architecture Items

  • Visual-inspection checks remain labeled as visual/manual evidence, not strict quantitative proof.
  • Several validation checks use proof-of-concept simplifications documented in SCRATCHPAD.md.
  • Retinal physiology settings here remain front-end assumptions only; no post-receptoral or cortical model is implied.

Architecture Coverage Matrix

Architecture itemImplemented testCode pathStatusNote
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctnessAngular Subtenseretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredAnalytic geometric equality check.
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across speciesRetinal Scaling Across Speciesretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredChecks focal-length proportionality.
Architecture §11e Accommodation/defocus injectionAccommodation Defocusretinal_sim/scene/geometry.pycoveredHard-cutoff accommodation model.
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor samplingDistance-Dependent Receptor Samplingretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredCounts stimulated receptors inside the projected object footprint.
Architecture §11a Metamer preservationMetamer Preservationretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pycoveredUses a fixed deterministic RGB-inferred reflectance regression dataset under D65.
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistencyRGB Round-Tripretinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.pycoveredUses the same D65 observer model as the reflectance-under-D65 basis construction for reprojection.
Architecture §11a Spectral response orderingSpectral Response Panelretinal_sim/validation/report.pycoveredUses deterministic RGB probes to sanity-check cone-catch ordering under the declared RGB-inferred D65 reconstruction model.
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolutionMTF vs Diffraction Limitretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredMeasures empirical sinusoidal contrast transfer against the implemented PSF MTF.
Architecture §11b PSF energy conservationPSF Energy Conservationretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredFloat64 normalization check.
Architecture §2a Pupil throughput and aperture geometryPupil Throughput Scalingretinal_sim/validation/report.pycoveredReport-layer pupil-area diagnostic normalised to the human reference pupil.
Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnosticsCat Slit Anisotropyretinal_sim/validation/report.pycoveredReport-layer elliptical Gaussian point-response diagnostic for the cat slit pupil.
Architecture §2b Longitudinal chromatic aberrationWavelength-Dependent Blurretinal_sim/optical/psf.pycoveredChecks signed LCA offsets and minimum blur near the 555 nm reference focus.
Architecture §2c Vitreous and media transmissionMedia Transmission Diagnosticsretinal_sim/optical/media.pycoveredSamples species media transmission tables and visualizes delivered spectra.
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity predictionSnellen Acuityretinal_sim/validation/acuity.pycoveredOrdering plus published-range checks.
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axisDichromat Confusionretinal_sim/validation/dichromat.pycoveredUses fixed confusion/control stimulus matrices with median-based comparisons.
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limitNyquist Samplingretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pycoveredConverts local cone density to cycles/degree using focal-length magnification.
Architecture §11c Receptor count validationReceptor Countretinal_sim/retina/mosaic.pycoveredCompares measured 1 mm² counts against density-model expectations.
Architecture §11d Known visual deficit reproductionColor Deficit Reproductionretinal_sim/pipeline.pycoveredEnd-to-end species comparison.
Architecture §11d Resolution gradientResolution Gradientretinal_sim/output/reconstruction.pycoveredQuantifies center-vs-periphery contrast loss on a checkerboard stimulus.

Open Code Review Findings

Read this report alongside CODEREVIEW.md and the generated status report. Validation pass/fail status does not replace code review findings or architecture audit notes.

Bonus: Reference Figures

Photoreceptor Mosaics

Test Details, Evidence, and Provenance

Angular Subtense PASS

analytic correctness strong
Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Angular subtense correctness
Expected outcome
2*arctan(w/(2d))
Observed outcome
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓ w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓ w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Absolute angular error < 0.01 degrees for every case.
Validation category
analytic correctness
Claim support level
strong
Evidence basis
Closed-form or invariant check against an analytic expectation.
External evidence summary
Closed-form geometry check with no external literature table required.
Claim scope note
Supports scene-geometry arithmetic only; it does not validate downstream retinal or perceptual behavior.
Inputs summary
Three analytic scene width/distance cases using the human optical model.
Method
Compare computed angular width against the closed-form geometric equation.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Exact 2*arctan geometry is the source of truth.
  • Human optical focal length is used only to build SceneDescription.
Limitations
  • Checks representative cases, not exhaustive parameter sweeps.
Artifacts
  • Bar chart: expected vs computed angular width
w=0.2m, d=1.0m → 11.4212° (expected 11.4212°) ✓
w=1.0m, d=10.0m → 5.7248° (expected 5.7248°) ✓
w=0.3m, d=6.0m → 2.8642° (expected 2.8642°) ✓

Retinal Scaling Across Species PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species
Expected outcome
retinal_width ∝ focal_length
Observed outcome
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓ cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Species ratios agree within 1% relative error.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to focal-length-based optical geometry expectations in the local external reference table.
Claim scope note
Supports focal-length-driven retinal image scaling only.
Inputs summary
One shared physical scene evaluated for human, dog, and cat species configs.
Method
Compare retinal_width_mm / focal_length_mm across species.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
  • claim_note
Assumptions
  • Retinal image width should scale with focal length when angular size is fixed.
Limitations
  • Only validates proportionality, not downstream sampling behavior.
Artifacts
  • Species focal-length bar chart
  • Species retinal-width bar chart
human: retinal_w=1.1150mm, focal=22.3mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
dog: retinal_w=0.8500mm, focal=17.0mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓
cat: retinal_w=0.9250mm, focal=18.5mm, ratio=0.050000 ✓

Accommodation Defocus PASS

analytic correctness strong
Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocus
Expected outcome
max(0, demand - max_accommodation)
Observed outcome
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓ dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓ cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓
Tolerance
0.1
Pass criterion
Residual defocus matches expected value within 0.1 diopters.
Validation category
analytic correctness
Claim support level
strong
Evidence basis
Closed-form or invariant check against an analytic expectation.
External evidence summary
Closed-form hard-cutoff accommodation check with no separate external evidence table.
Claim scope note
Supports the hard-cutoff accommodation rule currently implemented, not a full accommodation physiology model.
Inputs summary
Near-view scene at 0.25 m for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Compare computed residual defocus against max(0, demand - max_accommodation).
Code references
  • retinal_sim/scene/geometry.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Accommodation is a hard cutoff.
  • Max accommodation values are fixed PoC constants.
Limitations
  • No lead/lag accommodation response model is applied.
Artifacts
  • Expected vs computed residual defocus bars
human: demand=4.0D, max_acc=10.0D, residual=0.00D (expected 0.00D) ✓
dog: demand=4.0D, max_acc=2.5D, residual=1.50D (expected 1.50D) ✓
cat: demand=4.0D, max_acc=3.0D, residual=1.00D (expected 1.00D) ✓

Distance-Dependent Receptor Sampling PASS

model self-consistency moderate
Stage
scene
Architecture reference
Architecture §11e Distance-dependent receptor sampling
Expected outcome
~1/d² scaling
Observed outcome
d=1m → 51332 stimulated receptors d=2m → 9240 stimulated receptors d=4m → 2373 stimulated receptors d=8m → 575 stimulated receptors stimulated-count ratio (d=1.0m/d=8.0m): 89.3 vs expected 64.0 (rel err 0.39) ✓
Tolerance
0.4
Pass criterion
Far/near stimulated-receptor scaling stays within 40% relative error of ideal 1/d^2.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Checks inverse-square geometric consistency inside the current pipeline rather than direct external behavioral data.
Claim scope note
Supports geometric scaling behavior under current pipeline assumptions only.
Inputs summary
Constant-size grayscale object that remains smaller than the 2° patch across all tested distances.
Method
Measure stimulated receptor counts at multiple viewing distances and compare against ideal inverse-square scaling.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Stimulated receptors are those whose mapped positions fall inside the projected object footprint.
Limitations
  • Density gradients and stochastic mosaic sampling still introduce some deviation from exact inverse-square behaviour.
Artifacts
  • Distance vs stimulated receptor count plot
  • Log-log ideal vs measured scaling plot
d=1m → 51332 stimulated receptors
d=2m → 9240 stimulated receptors
d=4m → 2373 stimulated receptors
d=8m → 575 stimulated receptors
stimulated-count ratio (d=1.0m/d=8.0m): 89.3 vs expected 64.0 (rel err 0.39) ✓

Metamer Preservation PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Metamer preservation
Expected outcome
Max cone-catch relative difference < 3% for fixed near-metamer dataset
Observed outcome
cyan_blue_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0116 for [13, 150, 234] vs [52, 148, 235] ✓ teal_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0123 for [70, 192, 203] vs [106, 189, 203] ✓ green_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0280 for [61, 245, 158] vs [24, 249, 158] ✓ violet_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0331 for [127, 41, 209] vs [101, 60, 207] ✓
Tolerance
0.035
Pass criterion
Maximum cone-catch relative difference < 3.5% for every dataset pair.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Compared against a compact deterministic reference panel intended to approximate external metamer behavior under the declared scene assumptions.
Claim scope note
Supports a compact external-style regression panel under D65 assumptions only.
Inputs summary
Fixed near-metamer RGB pair dataset interpreted as RGB-inferred reflectance under D65 and integrated against human receptor sensitivities.
Method
Upsample each RGB pair in reflectance_under_d65 mode, integrate against human sensitivities, and bound the maximum cone-catch relative difference.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/opsin.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Dataset is repo-local and deterministic to keep the audit reproducible.
  • The spectra are inferred from RGB with the Smits-inspired D65-optimized reflectance model rather than measured directly.
Limitations
  • Dataset is compact rather than exhaustive; it is intended as a deterministic regression panel.
  • This is an RGB-inferred regression check, not evidence of uniquely recovered physical spectra.
Artifacts
  • Representative pair spectra plot
  • Per-pair max cone-catch difference bars
cyan_blue_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0116 for [13, 150, 234] vs [52, 148, 235] ✓
teal_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0123 for [70, 192, 203] vs [106, 189, 203] ✓
green_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0280 for [61, 245, 158] vs [24, 249, 158] ✓
violet_pair: max cone-catch rel diff=0.0331 for [127, 41, 209] vs [101, 60, 207] ✓

RGB Round-Trip PASS

model self-consistency moderate
Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Roundtrip consistency
Expected outcome
RMSE < 2.0 for all colours
Observed outcome
Max RMSE = 0.00
Tolerance
2.0
Pass criterion
RMSE < 2 8-bit counts for all tested colours.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Internal regression check for the declared RGB-inference path under D65 assumptions.
Claim scope note
Supports the current RGB-inferred reflectance reconstruction path, not unique recovery of scene spectra.
Inputs summary
Eight canonical sRGB colors passed through RGB-inferred reflectance reconstruction and D65-based reprojection.
Method
Project RGB-inferred reflectance spectra back to sRGB and compute per-colour RMSE.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • The same D65-referenced observer model used to build the basis spectra is used for reprojection.
  • This checks the declared reflectance_under_d65 inference path, not measured scene spectra.
Limitations
  • This validates the D65 round-trip path, not arbitrary illuminant changes.
Artifacts
  • RMSE bar chart
  • Input vs reprojected color swatches

Green: in=[0, 255, 0] out=[0,255,0] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Blue: in=[0, 0, 255] out=[0,0,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Yellow: in=[255, 255, 0] out=[255,255,0] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Cyan: in=[0, 255, 255] out=[0,255,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Magenta: in=[255, 0, 255] out=[255,0,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
White: in=[255, 255, 255] out=[255,255,255] RMSE=0.00 ✓
Gray: in=[128, 128, 128] out=[128,128,128] RMSE=0.00 ✓

Spectral Response Panel PASS

model self-consistency moderate
Stage
spectral
Architecture reference
Architecture §11a Spectral response ordering
Expected outcome
Anchor RGB probes preserve expected cone-catch dominance and blue-vs-warm separation across species
Observed outcome
human: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->M_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone human: sep(blue,yellow)=0.525, sep(blue,red)=0.583 dog: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone dog: sep(blue,yellow)=0.845, sep(blue,red)=0.793 cat: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone cat: sep(blue,yellow)=0.796, sep(blue,red)=0.799 OK: Human violet is S-dominant OK: Human blue is S-dominant OK: Human green is M-dominant OK: Human amber is L-dominant OK: Human red is L-dominant OK: Human yellow remains dominated by L+M cone catch OK: Human yellow keeps low S contribution relative to blue OK: Dog blue is S-dominant OK: Dog amber is L-dominant OK: Dog red is L-dominant OK: Dog blue-yellow separation remains substantial OK: Dog blue-red separation remains substantial OK: Cat blue is S-dominant OK: Cat amber is L-dominant OK: Cat red is L-dominant OK: Cat blue-yellow separation remains substantial OK: Cat blue-red separation remains substantial
Tolerance
0.45
Pass criterion
Anchor colors preserve expected cone-type dominance and dog/cat blue-vs-yellow/red separations exceed the fixed margin.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Internal response-order regression for the current D65-weighted RGB inference model.
Claim scope note
Supports ordering behavior in the declared RGB-inference model, not full spectral physiology.
Inputs summary
Deterministic RGB probe panel spanning violet-to-red anchors plus mixed colors, interpreted as RGB-inferred reflectance and integrated with D65-weighted species sensitivities.
Method
Upsample each probe color in reflectance_under_d65 mode, compute D65-weighted cone catches for each species, and verify expected dominance/order and blue-vs-warm separations.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/spectral/upsampler.py
  • retinal_sim/retina/opsin.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/datasets.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • The panel is RGB-defined and deterministic; it is a regression sanity check rather than a physical monochromator dataset.
  • Spectra are inferred from RGB using the reflectance_under_d65 model.
Limitations
  • Because the probes originate from sRGB values, this validates response ordering under the current D65-based reconstruction model rather than true narrowband physiology.
Artifacts
  • Representative RGB-inferred spectra plot
  • Per-species normalized cone-catch panel
human: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->M_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
human: sep(blue,yellow)=0.525, sep(blue,red)=0.583
dog: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
dog: sep(blue,yellow)=0.845, sep(blue,red)=0.793
cat: violet->S_cone, blue->S_cone, green->L_cone, yellow->L_cone, amber->L_cone, red->L_cone
cat: sep(blue,yellow)=0.796, sep(blue,red)=0.799
OK: Human violet is S-dominant
OK: Human blue is S-dominant
OK: Human green is M-dominant
OK: Human amber is L-dominant
OK: Human red is L-dominant
OK: Human yellow remains dominated by L+M cone catch
OK: Human yellow keeps low S contribution relative to blue
OK: Dog blue is S-dominant
OK: Dog amber is L-dominant
OK: Dog red is L-dominant
OK: Dog blue-yellow separation remains substantial
OK: Dog blue-red separation remains substantial
OK: Cat blue is S-dominant
OK: Cat amber is L-dominant
OK: Cat red is L-dominant
OK: Cat blue-yellow separation remains substantial
OK: Cat blue-red separation remains substantial

MTF vs Diffraction Limit PASS

model self-consistency moderate
Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §11b Diffraction-limited resolution
Expected outcome
Measured sinusoidal contrast transfer matches predicted MTF within 0.081
Observed outcome
Max |measured-predicted| = 0.0804
Tolerance
0.081
Pass criterion
Measured modulation stays within 0.08 of the predicted MTF at all tested spatial frequencies.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Compares measured modulation against the simulator's own implemented diffraction-derived PSF model.
Claim scope note
Supports the implemented optical approximation, not blanket validation of all anterior-eye optics.
Inputs summary
Human 550 nm PSF tested with sinusoidal gratings spanning low-to-mid spatial frequencies.
Method
Measure empirical contrast transfer on sinusoidal gratings and compare it with the PSF-predicted MTF.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • The implemented PSF is a diffraction-derived Gaussian approximation.
Limitations
  • Validation targets the implemented diffraction-derived PSF model, not a full Airy-pattern optical stack.
Artifacts

  • 550 nm PSF kernel image

Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.

Aligned to local pupil-area and focal-length expectation rows that describe modeled optical consequences by species.
Claim scope note
Supports the modeled optical throughput consequences of the configured pupil geometries only.

Human, dog, and cat species configs with circular/slit pupil geometries.

Compute pupil area for each species, normalize to the human reference pupil, and compare the resulting throughput ratios.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
  • claim_note
Assumptions
  • Pupil area is the relevant throughput proxy for the validation report.
  • Cat slit height is read from the species optical config when present.
Limitations
  • This is a report-layer optical-throughput diagnostic rather than an end-to-end irradiance measurement.
Artifacts
  • Species pupil area bar chart
  • Human-normalized throughput summary
human: pupil_shape=circular, pupil_area_mm2=7.0686, total_retinal_energy=1024.0000, throughput_vs_human=1.000, expected_ratio=1.000
dog: pupil_shape=circular, pupil_area_mm2=12.5664, total_retinal_energy=1820.4448, throughput_vs_human=1.778, expected_ratio=1.778
cat: pupil_shape=slit, pupil_area_mm2=10.9956, total_retinal_energy=1592.8888, throughput_vs_human=1.556, expected_ratio=1.556
Throughput is normalized to the human pupil area reference; the optical stage measurement uses a unit spectral field and a fixed scene scale.

Cat Slit Anisotropy PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnostics
Expected outcome
Cat slit pupil produces sigma_x > sigma_y and a wider horizontal cross-section
Observed outcome
sigma_x_px=2.149, sigma_y_px=1.011, FWHM_x_px=5, FWHM_y_px=3; psf_sigma_mm_x=0.0021, psf_sigma_mm_y=0.0010
Tolerance
1e-06
Pass criterion
Cat diagnostic reports sigma_x > sigma_y and FWHM_x > FWHM_y while remaining normalized.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to the local slit-pupil optical consequence table; evidence remains approximate because the model uses a slit approximation.
Claim scope note
Supports axis-aware blur reporting in the current slit approximation, not full feline ocular optics.
Inputs summary
Cat slit pupil evaluated with a point-response diagnostic at 550 nm.
Method
Generate a point-spread diagnostic, measure axis-specific sigma/FWHM, and require horizontal spread to exceed vertical spread.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/report.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
  • claim_note
Assumptions
  • The diagnostic uses an elliptical Gaussian approximation for the slit pupil.
  • Horizontal blur should exceed vertical blur for the vertical slit approximation.
Limitations
  • This report-layer approximation anticipates the axis-aware optical stage rather than replacing it.
Artifacts
  • Cat slit point-response heatmap
  • Axis-specific cross-section profile plot
cat pupil_shape=slit, pupil_area_mm2=10.9956
effective_f_number=4.944
effective_f_number_x=9.250
effective_f_number_y=2.643
sigma_x_mm=0.0021, sigma_y_mm=0.0010
sigma_x_px=2.149, sigma_y_px=1.011
psf_sigma_px_x=2.149, psf_sigma_px_y=1.011
FWHM_x_px=5, FWHM_y_px=3
anisotropy_active=True
psf_sigma_mm_x=0.0021
Point-response diagnostic uses the current optical-stage slit kernel and metadata.

Wavelength-Dependent Blur PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §2b Longitudinal chromatic aberration
Expected outcome
Short and long wavelengths carry opposite-signed LCA offsets and the smallest PSF width occurs near 555 nm
Observed outcome
human: lca_400=+1.000D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-1.000D, min_sigma_at=555nm ✓ dog: lca_400=+0.750D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-0.750D, min_sigma_at=555nm ✓ cat: lca_400=+0.750D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-0.750D, min_sigma_at=550nm ✓
Tolerance
0.05
Pass criterion
Offsets are positive in the short wavelengths, negative in the long wavelengths, and minimum PSF width occurs near 555 nm for each species.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to local wavelength-delivery and optical-behavior reference assumptions rather than direct in-vivo PSF measurements.
Claim scope note
Supports the current wavelength-aware optical model and its assumptions, not full empirical PSF validation.
Inputs summary
Canonical wavelength grid evaluated for all three species using the current Gaussian-plus-LCA optical model.
Method
Sample per-wavelength LCA offsets and PSF widths from the optical-stage metadata and verify sign/order behavior.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/psf.py
  • retinal_sim/optical/stage.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
  • global_note
Assumptions
  • R2 models LCA as a signed wavelength-dependent defocus offset around a 555 nm reference focus.
  • The smallest blur should occur near the reference wavelength when scene defocus is zero.
Limitations
  • This validates the implemented R2 operational model, not a full empirical aberration fit.
Artifacts
  • Wavelength vs LCA offset plot
  • Wavelength vs PSF sigma plot
human: lca_400=+1.000D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-1.000D, min_sigma_at=555nm ✓
dog: lca_400=+0.750D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-0.750D, min_sigma_at=555nm ✓
cat: lca_400=+0.750D, lca_555=+0.000D, lca_700=-0.750D, min_sigma_at=550nm ✓

Media Transmission Diagnostics PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
optical
Architecture reference
Architecture §2c Vitreous and media transmission
Expected outcome
Each species attenuates short wavelengths more than long wavelengths and the sampled transmission curves are species-specific
Observed outcome
human: blue_mean=0.424, red_mean=0.990, source=C:\Users\steve\retinal_sim\retinal_sim\data\optical_media\human_reference.csv ✓ dog: blue_mean=0.742, red_mean=0.991, source=C:\Users\steve\retinal_sim\retinal_sim\data\optical_media\dog_reference.csv ✓ cat: blue_mean=0.792, red_mean=0.993, source=C:\Users\steve\retinal_sim\retinal_sim\data\optical_media\cat_reference.csv ✓ Species transmission curves are distinct: ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species transmits less blue than red and the sampled transmission curves differ across species.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to the configured species ocular-media reference curves and their expected shortwave-vs-longwave behavior.
Claim scope note
Supports the configured species transmission assumptions, not full individualized ocular-media physiology.
Inputs summary
Species ocular-media transmission tables sampled on the canonical wavelength grid and applied to a unit spectrum.
Method
Sample the configured transmission curves, compare short-vs-long wavelength attenuation, and visualize the delivered spectra.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/optical/media.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
  • retinal_sim/optical/stage.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
  • global_note
Assumptions
  • R2 uses fixed species reference transmission curves rather than age- or state-dependent media variants.
  • Short-wavelength attenuation should exceed long-wavelength attenuation for each species.
Limitations
  • Does not model scatter or age-dependent lens brunescence in R2.
Artifacts
  • Species transmission curve plot
  • Delivered-spectrum plot after ocular media

dog: blue_mean=0.742, red_mean=0.991, source=C:\Users\steve\retinal_sim\retinal_sim\data\optical_media\dog_reference.csv ✓

Snellen Acuity PASS

external empirical alignment moderate
Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Snellen acuity prediction
Expected outcome
human < dog ≈ cat; within published ranges
Observed outcome
human=2.0′, dog=8.0′, cat=8.0′
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species falls within its target range and human acuity is best.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to published species acuity target ranges stored locally for deterministic reporting.
Claim scope note
Supports approximate species acuity alignment at the retinal front end, not full behavioral or cortical vision validation.
Inputs summary
Species-specific acuity prediction using Snellen E orientation discriminability.
Method
Predict minimum resolvable angular size for each species and compare with expected ranges and ordering.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/acuity.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/snellen.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
Assumptions
  • Published acuity ranges are broad PoC acceptance bands.
  • Letter-region masking and Pearson correlation distance are required.
Limitations
  • Cat predictions can be seed-sensitive near threshold.
Artifacts
  • Snellen stimulus examples
  • Predicted acuity bar chart
human: predicted 2.0 arcmin (expected 1.0–5.0) ✓
dog: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
cat: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
Ordering human ≤ dog ≤ cat: human=2.0, dog=8.0, cat=8.0 ✓

Dichromat Confusion PASS

external empirical alignment weak
Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Dichromat confusion axis
Expected outcome
Species-specific confusion medians are suppressed relative to controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set
Observed outcome
human medians: dog-conf=0.0374, cat-conf=0.1191, control=0.3653 dog medians: dog-conf=0.0301, cat-conf=0.0972, control=0.3275 cat medians: dog-conf=0.0498, cat-conf=0.0140, control=0.2164 Dog confusion median < dog control median: ✓ Cat confusion median < cat control median: ✓ Human > cat on cat confusion set: ✓ Dog control median > 0.05: ✓ Cat control median > 0.05: ✓
Tolerance
0.01
Pass criterion
Each species-specific confusion median is suppressed relative to its controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
weak
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned indirectly to expected species confusion-axis behavior; evidence is illustrative rather than exhaustive.
Claim scope note
Supports qualitative species confusion-axis differences at the retinal front end, not complete color appearance prediction.
Inputs summary
Fixed confusion-pair panels for dog and cat plus a shared control-pair panel.
Method
Compute species-wise discriminability distributions across deterministic confusion/control stimulus matrices and compare confusion medians against controls.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/validation/dichromat.py
  • retinal_sim/validation/ishihara.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Stimulus scaling is applied to keep Naka-Rushton responses out of saturation.
Limitations
  • Compact panel intended for deterministic regression rather than exhaustive colour-space coverage.
Artifacts
  • Median discriminability bar chart
human medians: dog-conf=0.0374, cat-conf=0.1191, control=0.3653
dog medians: dog-conf=0.0301, cat-conf=0.0972, control=0.3275
cat medians: dog-conf=0.0498, cat-conf=0.0140, control=0.2164

Cat confusion median < cat control median: ✓

Nyquist Sampling PASS

external empirical alignment strong
Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
Expected outcome
Species Nyquist estimates fall within target cpd bands
Observed outcome
human: cone_density=200000/mm², spacing=2.40µm, nyquist=81.0 cpd (target 45-90) ✓ dog: cone_density=12000/mm², spacing=9.81µm, nyquist=15.1 cpd (target 10-30) ✓ cat: cone_density=10000/mm², spacing=10.75µm, nyquist=15.0 cpd (target 8-25) ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Each species falls inside its target cycles/degree band.
Validation category
external empirical alignment
Claim support level
strong
Evidence basis
Comparison against a local deterministic reference table representing published or externally grounded expectations.
External evidence summary
Aligned to density-derived species Nyquist target ranges stored locally for deterministic reporting.
Claim scope note
Supports density-derived front-end sampling limits, not full perceptual acuity validation.
Inputs summary
Area-centralis cone densities converted to spacing and cycles/degree for all species.
Method
Convert local cone density to spacing, then convert spacing to cycles/degree using focal-length-based retinal magnification.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
  • human
  • dog
  • cat
Assumptions
  • Hexagonal spacing approximation is used to map cone density to Nyquist limit.
Limitations
  • Uses density-model predictions at the patch centre rather than a full eccentricity sweep.
Artifacts
  • Species Nyquist-limit bar chart
human: cone_density=200000/mm², spacing=2.40µm, nyquist=81.0 cpd (target 45-90) ✓
dog: cone_density=12000/mm², spacing=9.81µm, nyquist=15.1 cpd (target 10-30) ✓
cat: cone_density=10000/mm², spacing=10.75µm, nyquist=15.0 cpd (target 8-25) ✓

Receptor Count PASS

model self-consistency moderate
Stage
retinal
Architecture reference
Architecture §11c Receptor count validation
Expected outcome
Measured counts and cone/rod ratios stay within density-model tolerance bands over the simulated patch
Observed outcome
human: measured=191176, expected=191233, count_err=0.000, cone_ratio_err=0.004 ✓ dog: measured=144374, expected=144474, count_err=0.001, cone_ratio_err=0.003 ✓ cat: measured=191710, expected=192048, count_err=0.002, cone_ratio_err=0.010 ✓
Tolerance
0.25
Pass criterion
Total count error < 25% and cone/rod composition error < 15% for every species.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
moderate
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Compares generated mosaics against the project's density-model expectations; evidence remains indirect until tied to richer external histology tables.
Claim scope note
Supports the current mosaic-density implementation at the retinal front end, not full retinal histology.
Inputs summary
Generated mosaics compared against density-model expectations over the simulated square patch.
Method
Compare measured receptor counts and cone/rod composition against integrated density-model expectations over the simulated patch.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/retina/mosaic.py
  • retinal_sim/species/config.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Species density functions are the literature-backed PoC source of truth for expected counts.
Limitations
  • Current model still uses simplified analytic density functions rather than digitised histology maps.
Artifacts
  • Measured vs expected count bars
human: measured=191176, expected=191233, count_err=0.000, cone_ratio_err=0.004 ✓
dog: measured=144374, expected=144474, count_err=0.001, cone_ratio_err=0.003 ✓
cat: measured=191710, expected=192048, count_err=0.002, cone_ratio_err=0.010 ✓

Color Deficit Reproduction PASS

model self-consistency weak
Stage
e2e
Architecture reference
Architecture §11d Known visual deficit reproduction
Expected outcome
Human sees R/G difference; dog does not
Observed outcome
human Δ=0.2634, dog Δ=0.1602
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Human left/right difference exceeds dog by at least 20%.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
weak
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Proxy end-to-end comparison that illustrates retained information differences without claiming perceptual equivalence.
Claim scope note
Illustrates comparative retinal-information loss and should not be read as direct conscious appearance.
Inputs summary
Red-green split stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Measure left/right cone-response difference after full-pipeline simulation.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/output/voronoi.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Left/right response norm is used as the summary metric.
Limitations
  • This is a compact proxy for a richer end-to-end color-deficit comparison.
Artifacts
  • Input stimulus
  • Per-species comparative renderings
human: L/R diff = 0.2634
dog: L/R diff = 0.1602
cat: L/R diff = 0.1961
Human > Dog*1.2: ✓

Resolution Gradient PASS

model self-consistency weak
Stage
e2e
Architecture reference
Architecture §11d Resolution gradient
Expected outcome
Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and dichromat center contrast on a fine checkerboard
Observed outcome
human: center_contrast=0.0143, periphery_contrast=0.0122 dog: center_contrast=0.0062, periphery_contrast=0.0063 cat: center_contrast=0.0038, periphery_contrast=0.0037 Human center > human periphery: ✓ Human center > dog center: ✓ Human center > cat center: ✓
Tolerance
0.0
Pass criterion
Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and exceeds dog/cat center contrast.
Validation category
model self-consistency
Claim support level
weak
Evidence basis
Internal regression or consistency check within the simulator's declared implementation and assumptions.
External evidence summary
Proxy end-to-end contrast-retention check, not a full empirical eccentricity validation.
Claim scope note
Illustrates front-end contrast retention trends and should not be read as a full perceptual validation.
Inputs summary
Checkerboard stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
Method
Compare center vs peripheral bright/dark response contrast on a fine checkerboard and compare human center contrast against dichromat center contrast.
Code references
  • retinal_sim/pipeline.py
  • retinal_sim/output/reconstruction.py
  • retinal_sim/output/voronoi.py
External reference table
None recorded.
Assumptions
  • Checkerboard bright/dark contrast is used as the resolvability proxy.
Limitations
  • Quantifies contrast retention on a fine checkerboard but does not yet fit a full eccentricity-dependent MTF curve.
Artifacts
  • Input checkerboard
  • Per-species comparative renderings
  • Per-species retinal-information renderings
human: center_contrast=0.0143, periphery_contrast=0.0122
dog: center_contrast=0.0062, periphery_contrast=0.0063
cat: center_contrast=0.0038, periphery_contrast=0.0037
